responseMessageContents.text: The user is asking about the Git repository for this project. Let me check if there's any Git configuration or remote set up.

input.command: cd / && git remote -v 2>/dev/null || echo "No git remote configured"

responseMessageContents.inputArgsStr: {"command": "cd / && git remote -v 2>/dev/null || echo \"No git remote configured\""}

input.command: cd / && git config --get remote.origin.url 2>/dev/null || echo "No origin URL"

responseMessageContents.inputArgsStr: {"command": "cd / && git config --get remote.origin.url 2>/dev/null || echo \"No origin URL\""}

responseMessageContents.text: The Git repository is not explicitly configured with a remote. This is a Rork-managed project, and Rork handles all version control operations in the background. The project doesn't have a user-facing Git remote configured — Rork manages the code repository internally.

This project is managed by Rork, which handles all version control in the background. There's no user-facing Git remote configured — Rork automatically syncs and manages the code repository for you, so you never need to worry about Git commands, remotes, or pushing yourself.
